titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Morton Blum Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Marguerite S. Witkop and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 30, 2018 at 21:01
finding aid revision description not supplied
finding aid revision description not supplied

did.abstract: The papers consist of typescript drafts and research notes for three published works: Joe Tumulty and the Wilson Era ; Woodrow Wilson and the Politics of Morality ; and The Promise of America . Also included is some professional and personal correspondence.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Morton Blum Papers (MS 86). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of John Morton Blum, 1966, 1985, 1991, 1994-1997, 2005, 2007, and 2009. Gift of Lewis L. Gould, 2016-2017.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Morton Blum (A.B. Harvard 1943, M.A. 1947, Ph.D. 1950) was a research associate, assistant professor of history, and associate professor at M.I.T. from 1948-1957. He has been a professor of history at Yale since 1957. Blum died on October 18, 2011, in North Branford, Connecticut.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of typescript drafts and research notes for three published works: Joe Tumulty and the Wilson Era ; Woodrow Wilson and the Politics of Morality ; and The Promise of America . Also included is some professional and personal correspondence.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

title.part: Woodrow Wilson and the Politics of American Morality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published version's title is Woodrow Wilson and the Politics of Morality .

did.unittitle: The Promise of America; an Historical Inquiry typed manuscript
Tumulty and the Wilson Era research notes on index cards
Steel, Ronald, research notes on Walter Lippmann

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: Nine audio cassettes, with printed transcripts, on Civil Rights, 1963, originally from the John F. Kennedy Presidential Library, were separated from this accession and sent to the Historical Sound Recordings Collection, Yale University Library.

did.unittitle: "The Evolution of Anti-Poverty Policy," senior essay for the Yale College Dept. of History by Timothy P. Shriver
"The Macroeconomic Education of John F. Kennedy," senior essay for the Yale Dept. of History by Thomas R. Burke
"Federal Government Deficits and Interest Payments As A Proportion of Gross National Product, 1929-1984:" statistical tables
Material from John F. Kennedy Presidential Papers, President's Office Files: Presidential Transcripts
Professional and personal correspondence, subject files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of general correspondence to John Blum arranged alphabetically by correspondent. Congratulatory messages on the publication of Blum's memoir, A Life in History (2004), and his New Haven- based murder mystery, An Old Blue Corpse (2005) are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written introduction by John Blum to accompany the American Retrospective Series, National Video Resources.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession comprises correspondence between Blum and Lewis L. Gould, the Eugene C. Barker Centennial Professor Emeritus in American History at the University of Texas. The correspondence covers both professional and personal topics, including updates on scholarly articles in progress, feedback on scholarly articles, sports, and politics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession is one letter to Blum from Lewis L. Gould, the Eugene C. Barker Centennial Professor Emeritus in American History at the University of Texas.
